description:
Download sequence databases from the internet in formats appropriate for Domainator
    Including the following databases:
    - Uniprot (includes Trembl and SwissProt) (downloads fasta by default, use _gb suffix to download genbank file with addtional annotations)
    - Trembl (downloads fasta by default, use _gb suffix to download genbank file with addtional annotations)
    - SwissProt (downloads fasta by default, use _gb suffix to download genbank file with addtional annotations)
    - ncbi_complete_genome_proks: NCBI genomes where assembly_level is "Complete Genome". (excluding eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_complete_genome_nonredundant_proks: non-redundant NCBI genomes where assembly_level is "Complete Genome". (excluding eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_representative_proks : representative and reference genomes from the NCBI database (excluding eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_representative_all : representative and reference genomes from the NCBI database (all taxa, including eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_nonredundant_proks : non-redundant genomes from the NCBI database (excluding eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_nonredundant_all : non-redundant genomes from the NCBI database (all taxa, including eukaryotes)
    - ncbi_all : all genomes from the NCBI database including duplicates of the same species (all taxa, including eukaryotes). Can produce very large files, up to 10 TB or more, so use with caution.
 

    Key taxa:
        81490 : prokaryotic environmental samples
        408169 : metagenomes
        2 : bacteria
        2157 : archaea
        10239 : viruses
        
        2759 : eukaryota


WARNING: Downloading large databases can take a long time and use a lot of disk space!

parameters.name: Include Taxids
parameters.help: Space separated list of taxids to include

parameters.name: Exclude Taxids
parameters.help: Space separated list of taxids to exclude

parameters.name: Taxonomy Expr
parameters.help: A boolean expression over taxids using operators & (AND), | (OR), ~ (NOT), and parentheses, e.g. '2 & ~1224' (within Bacteria but not Proteobacteria). A taxid is true when it appears in the lineage. Mutually exclusive with include/exclude taxids. For prokaryote-only (_proks) databases, Eukaryota are excluded in addition.

parameters.name: Ncbi Taxonomy Path
parameters.help: Path to NCBI taxonomy database directory. Will be created and downloaded if it does not exist.

parameters.name: Skip Taxonomy Update
parameters.help: By default, if taxonomy database exists, check it against the version on the ncbi server and update if there is a newer version. Setting this flag will skip this check.

parameters.name: Success Rec Log
parameters.help: Path to log file for successfully written records. If not specified, successfully written records will not be logged.

parameters.name: Exclude Accessions
parameters.help: Exclude genbank records with these accessions. Should be versionless (i.e. without the '.' and number after the '.')

parameters.name: Exclude Accessions File
parameters.help: text file containing accessions genbank records to exclude. Should be versionless (i.e. without the '.' and number after the '.')

parameters.name: Ncbi Summary
parameters.help: Path to NCBI summary file. Normally it will be downloaded from the Internet on every execution, but you may want to pre-download it, or make custom subsets, the url is: https://ftp.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/genomes/genbank/assembly_summary_genbank.txt.

parameters.name: Gene Call
parameters.help: When activated, for nucleotide databases, such as Genbank, new CDS annotations will be added with Prodigal in Metagenomic mode. If 'all', then any existing CDS annotations will be deleted and all contigs will be re-annotated. If 'unannotated', then only contigs without CDS annotations will be annotated. [default: None].

parameters.name: Num Recs
parameters.help: Stop after downloading this many records. For UniProt, this is the number of proteins. For genbank, the number of genomes, including multi-contig genomes. This is mainly for testing.

parameters.name: Cpu
parameters.help: the number of cores of the cpu which are used at a time to download ncbi records [default: use all available cores]

parameters.name: Download Backend
parameters.help: Network backend for NCBI genome downloads. 'datasets' uses the NCBI Datasets CLI dehydrate/rehydrate workflow (recommended for large jobs; NCBI's own bulk tool handles throttling). 'direct' downloads .gbff.gz files over HTTPS directly.

advanced_parameters.name: Download Workers
advanced_parameters.help: For the 'direct' backend: number of simultaneous HTTPS connections to the NCBI genomes server. NCBI rate-limits by concurrency, so keep this low (~3). 0 = use --cpu.

advanced_parameters.name: Email
advanced_parameters.help: Contact email included in the User-Agent header sent to NCBI (politeness; not an API key). Falls back to the NCBI_EMAIL environment variable.

advanced_parameters.name: Ncbi Api Key
advanced_parameters.help: NCBI API key passed to the 'datasets' backend (does not affect the 'direct' backend / raw FTP server). Falls back to the NCBI_API_KEY environment variable.

advanced_parameters.name: Datasets Workdir
advanced_parameters.help: For the 'datasets' backend: working directory for the dehydrated package and rehydrated files. Required when --download_backend is 'datasets'.

advanced_parameters.name: Datasets Include
advanced_parameters.help: For the 'datasets' backend: value passed to `datasets ... --include`.

advanced_parameters.name: Datasets Max Workers
advanced_parameters.help: For the 'datasets' backend: value passed to `datasets rehydrate --max-workers`. Keep low to be NCBI-friendly.

advanced_parameters.name: Datasets Path
advanced_parameters.help: Path to the `datasets` (NCBI Datasets CLI) binary. If not given, it is looked up on PATH.

advanced_parameters.name: Before
advanced_parameters.help: For ncbi databases, only download records before this date. Format is YYYY-MM-DD.

advanced_parameters.name: After
advanced_parameters.help: For ncbi databases, only download records after this date. Format is YYYY-MM-DD.
